commit.message:
feat(benchmarks): add conv_cifar_v5 suite with cutout, more filters, and hierarchical stages

Add cutout augmentation helpers (_augment_cutout, _augment_flip_cutout)
for masking random 8×8 patches as a complementary regularization
strategy to horizontal flip.

Build conv_cifar_v5 suite (12 configs) exploring five directions:
- ×20 ensembles at 20k neurons (does scaling continue past ×10?)
- More filters per branch: 48×3 (144 features) and 64×3 (192 features)
  at 20k neurons with ×5 and ×10 ensembles — cleanly testing whether
  richer convolutional features help, without GCN which hurt in v2
- Cutout augmentation (flip+cutout) as alternative to hflip-only
- Two-stage hierarchical pipelines: 64f p5→128f p3 sequential stages
  to capture higher-order patterns from first-stage feature maps
- 30k neurons with stronger regularization (alpha=2e-2) to test
  whether the 12% train-test gap can be reduced at higher capacity

Update colab_launcher.ipynb with v5 suite entry.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

﻿CADExchange: 修复 geometry sidecar 缺少 length_unit 字段，跨系统比较只能退回 raw 坐标，容差在 mm 与 m 场景下会被错误解释。

【问题详情与根因】
geometry sidecar 缺少 length_unit 字段，跨系统比较只能退回 raw 坐标，容差在 mm 与 m 场景下会被错误解释。

【修复方案与实现】
在 GeometryCollectorBase.SaveEdgesToJson 增加可选参数 lengthUnit，并在 geometry json 顶层按需写入 ""length_unit"" 字段。默认参数为空，历史调用保持兼容，不改变旧流程行为；新调用可显式传入 m/mm/in 等单位令牌，确保 sidecar 与主模型单位一致。

commit.message:
﻿CADExchange: 修复 Revolve 轴在引用草图段时仅写 RefLocalID，缺少独立 ReferenceEntity 节点，导致引用结构与 Edge/Face 不统一，跨端解析时无法稳定还原父草图与段信息。

【问题详情与根因】
Revolve 轴在引用草图段时仅写 RefLocalID，缺少独立 ReferenceEntity 节点，导致引用结构与 Edge/Face 不统一，跨端解析时无法稳定还原父草图与段信息。

【修复方案与实现】
在 TinyXMLSerializer 的 Revolve Axis 序列化路径中，当存在 RefLocalID 且未提供 referenceEntity 时自动补写 Type=SketchSeg 的 ReferenceEntity（ParentFeatureID 与 SegmentLocalID）；在反序列化路径中识别 SketchSeg 并回填 RefLocalID，保持旧写侧行为兼容。同时在 MigrationRegressionTest 新增回归用例，覆盖保存与读取链路，确保后续重构不会回退此语义。`n`nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refactor transactions, metrics and BTree internals

Expose optional ITransaction parameters across collection APIs and remove implicit transaction acquisition via transaction holder; default to engine/local null transaction id where appropriate. Commit pruning transactions explicitly. Add defensive rollback on auto-commit failures. Instrument many collection query paths with ValueStopwatch and publish MetricEvent for collection queries.

Update IDocumentCollection interface with overloads for FindByIdAsync/FindAllAsync/FindAsync/FindOneAsync/QueryIndexAsync/ScanAsync to accept an optional ITransaction. Adjust DynamicCollection and DocumentCollection implementations to propagate optional transactions and to use explicit transaction begin/commit where needed.

Major BTreeIndex improvements: lower MaxEntriesPerNode to 64 and replace linear scans with zero-allocation binary-search and offset-table approaches (stackalloc offsets) for internal traversal, leaf lookup, insertion, deletion, split and underflow/borrow operations. Use in-place/streamed copies instead of temporary allocations for shifting entry data and optimize page writes. These changes aim to improve performance, reduce allocations and make index operations more robust.

Tests updated accordingly.

commit.message:
refactor(grid): restructure into base/cartesian/spherical/cylindrical layout (#207)

* refactor(grid): restructure into base / cartesian / spherical / cylindrical layout

Split the monolithic _src/grid module into a layered hierarchy mirroring
finitevolX's coordinate-system extensibility goals:

  ArakawaCGrid1D/2D/3D       (base.py)  pure topology, just Nx/Ny/Nz
      └─ CurvilinearGrid1D/2D/3D        adds uniform metric (Lx, dx, ...)
          ├─ CartesianGrid1D/2D/3D      cartesian.py  (concrete, was ArakawaCGrid*)
          ├─ SphericalGrid2D/3D         spherical.py  (concrete, was SphericalArakawaCGrid*)
          └─ CylindricalGrid2D/3D       cylindrical.py  (TODO stub)

Hard renames (no shims):
  ArakawaCGrid1D/2D/3D       -> CartesianGrid1D/2D/3D
  SphericalArakawaCGrid2D/3D -> SphericalGrid2D/3D

The names ArakawaCGrid1D/2D/3D are reused for the new abstract topology
layer. SphericalGrid* are now siblings of CartesianGrid* under CurvilinearGrid*
rather than subclasses, eliminating the previous "spherical IS-A cartesian"
inheritance accident.

Adjacent moves (single-file, content unchanged):
  _src/grid/cgrid_mask.py    -> _src/mask/cgrid_mask.py
  _src/grid/constants.py     -> _src/utils/constants.py

Type annotations:
  Interpolation1D/2D/3D widened from CartesianGrid* to CurvilinearGrid*
  to preserve the previous semantics where SphericalDivergence2D
  internally constructs Interpolation2D(grid=spherical_grid). Pure
  averaging is coordinate-agnostic and Interpolation* doesn't access
  any grid metric fields.

  can subclass the abstract layers directly.

advection, diffusion, solvers, vertical, mask, tests, scripts, notebooks).

* fix(velox): Handle generic-typed companion function lookup for collect_set/list

companion functions with suffix using generic type variables (e.g.,
collect_set_merge_extract_array_T). The Substrait layer was constructing
concrete type suffixes (e.g., array_row_VARCHAR_BIGINT_BIGINT_endrow) that
don't match.

companion function names via getCompanionFunctionSignatures() API.

commit.message:
Feature/enterprise migration (#33)

* feat: migrate enterprise features to BSL 1.1 enterprise/ directory

Implements PRODUCT_ARCHITECTURE.md v0.12.0 -> v0.13.0 migration plan.
Establishes dual-license structure: MIT core + BSL 1.1 enterprise.

What moved to enterprise/:
- Auth infrastructure: API key stores, JWT/OIDC, RBAC, OPA, rate limiter,
  SQLite/Postgres/event-sourced stores (enterprise/auth/infrastructure/)
- Auth CQRS: commands, handlers, queries (enterprise/auth/commands/, queries/)
- Auth REST endpoints (enterprise/auth/api/routes.py)
- Auth bootstrap + config + CLI + HTTP middleware (enterprise/auth/)
- Role definitions (enterprise/auth/roles.py)
- SQLite event store (enterprise/persistence/sqlite_event_store.py)
- Langfuse observability adapter (enterprise/integrations/langfuse.py)
- All auth and Langfuse tests (enterprise/tests/)

What stays in core (MIT):
- All domain contracts and value objects
- All application ports (ObservabilityPort, etc.)
- InMemoryEventStore
- Domain state machine, circuit breaker, health checks
- Prometheus metrics, OTEL export
- REST API infrastructure (minus auth routes)
- CLI, hot-reload, batch invocations

Import boundary enforcement:
- scripts/check_enterprise_boundary.sh (CI check)
- Rule 1 (hard): domain/ has zero enterprise imports
- Rule 2 (hard): no unconditional enterprise imports outside designated shims
- Rule 3 (hard): operator/helm-charts never import enterprise
- Core shims conditionally re-export from enterprise (try/except guards)
- Bootstrap conditionally loads enterprise auth, event store, observability

Compatibility:
- Core works standalone without enterprise (auth disabled, in-memory event store)
- enterprise/ added to pytest pythonpath for test discovery
- All 2447 core unit tests pass

License:
- Root LICENSE updated: MIT scope clarified, references enterprise/LICENSE.BSL
- enterprise/LICENSE.BSL: BSL 1.1 (change date 3 years, change license MIT)

* refactor: move packages/core to src/ layout per PRODUCT_ARCHITECTURE.md
Implements structural reorganisation from PRODUCT_ARCHITECTURE.md section 8.
The Python core package no longer lives in packages/core/ -- it is now at
the repository root in standard src/ layout.
Directory changes:
- packages/core/mcp_hangar/ -> src/mcp_hangar/
- packages/core/tests/     -> tests/
- packages/core/Dockerfile  -> Dockerfile
Configuration changes:
- Root pyproject.toml now contains the real mcp-hangar package config
  (replaces placeholder that previously pointed to packages/core/)
- [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] packages = ["src/mcp_hangar"]
- [tool.pytest.ini_options] pythonpath = ["src", "."]
CI/tooling updates:
- .github/workflows/ci-core.yml: paths src/**, pythonpath, artifact path dist/
- .github/workflows/release.yml: working-directory ., dist/ paths, Dockerfile path
- .github/workflows/version-bump.yml: pyproject.toml at root
- .github/workflows/security.yml: pip install -e .
- .github/workflows/pr-validation.yml: path filter src/**
- .github/workflows/ci-docs.yml: *.md at root
- Makefile: all targets now run from repo root
- scripts/check_enterprise_boundary.sh: src/ instead of packages/core/
Why:
- PRODUCT_ARCHITECTURE.md target structure uses src/mcp_hangar/ at root
- Standard Python src/ layout is more conventional
- Removes redundant packages/core/ indirection for the primary package
- packages/ directory now contains only operator/, ui/, helm-charts/
  (non-Python subprojects that have their own build systems)
packages/core/ now contains only README.md explaining the move.
Result:
- 2446 unit tests pass from repo root
- mcp_hangar importable via src/ layout
- enterprise/ accessible via pythonpath = ["src", "."]
